params.prompt: Vertical portrait brand social tile for a barbershop, exact 4:5 aspect ratio, 1080x1350 Instagram feed post. Background: high-contrast black and white editorial photography, duotone grading with a warm golden-yellow rim/key light used only as an implied light source, never a flat color wash, no straight color photography. Scene: wide shot inside a barbershop's grooming spa corner -- a confident male client seated in a barber chair, one hand resting in a small stainless steel bowl of soapy water for a manicure, his bare foot already resting on a folded white towel nearby ready for a pedicure, nail tools (file, buffer, cuticle pusher) neatly arranged on the towel, moody duotone barbershop interior atmosphere in the soft-focus background, unhurried and professional mood. Add a dark ink (#101010) gradient scrim across the lower half for text legibility. Top-left: a small solid Nordic-yellow (#FFD200) pill-shaped tag containing bold condensed uppercase sans-serif text "CUIDADO DE MANOS Y PIES" in dark ink letters, tight letter tracking. Center-lower area: a large headline reading "PIES Y MANOS A PUNTO", set in the hand-cut marker lettering style shown in the second reference image (a font character map) -- reproduce that exact letterform style, all uppercase, tight line height -- where the word PUNTO is solid Nordic yellow (#FFD200) and the rest of the words are solid bone-white (#F7F4EC), no other colors on the headline. Directly below the headline, one short line of clean condensed grotesk sans-serif body text in bone-white at reduced opacity, reading: "Manicura y pedicura para hombres, sin excusas. Desliza y conoce el proceso." Bottom-right corner: place the exact logo lockup shown in the first reference image (a white Viking helmet mark beside the words NORDIC / Barber Shop, already on a black background) completely unchanged and at small watermark scale -- do not invent, redraw, alter proportions, recolor, or add glow/shadow to this logo; blend its black background seamlessly into the tile's own ink-black canvas so no visible box or seam appears around it. Keep the overall canvas ink-black (#101010) dominant, yellow only as a small accent never exceeding about 10-15% of the frame.
